description:
Trigger Unity script recompilation and wait for it to complete. Unity's auto domain reload is disabled by default — after modifying .cs files you MUST call this once to apply your code changes, always before relying on them in unity_execute. On success a domain reload occurs and the Unity plugin reconnects automatically; on failure compile errors are returned.

To merely check for compile errors, prefer code_diagnostics — it is fast and does not touch Unity. This tool brings the Unity Editor to the foreground and may take 10-60 seconds depending on project size. Do NOT call it while `editor_status` is `playing` or `playing_paused`.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Unity project root. Required.

editor_status.description: The canonical Unity Editor status from the Environment section's `Unity Editor Status`. Allowed values: `disconnected`, `editing`, `playing`, `playing_paused`. You MUST read the current status and pass it exactly. `unity_recompile` only runs when the actual status is `editing`.
